commonIssues:
The 'Pehchan' Rajasthan portal frequently experiences server downtime, causing delays in filing new applications or submitting corrections, especially in municipal corporation areas.

How to fix:

  • Try the Pehchan Portal during early morning hours (6–9 AM) for lower server load.
  • Alternatively, use the eMitra Portal or visit your nearest eMitra kiosk for assisted application.
  • If both portals are down, submit the application offline at the local ULB/Gram Panchayat office and get a stamped acknowledgement.
  • Helpline: 181 (Rajasthan Sampark) | eMitra: 1800-180-6127

Deaths must be reported within 21 days. Delays require a late fee, affidavits, and in some cases magistrate approval, complicating the process particularly for home deaths.

How to fix:

  • 21–30 days: Apply at the local ULB/Gram Panchayat with a late fee.
  • 30 days to 1 year: Submit a Notarised Affidavit with an application for special permission to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM).
  • After 1 year: Obtain an order from the Executive Magistrate / SDM, then apply at the local registrar office.
  • Apply via Pehchan Portal or eMitra.

Lack of proper documentation — such as the medical certificate of cause of death — or errors in the affidavit regarding date/place of death lead to rejection.

How to fix:

  • Obtain the MCCD (Medical Certificate of Cause of Death) from the hospital — this is the primary required document.
  • For home deaths, get a certificate from a Registered Medical Practitioner (RMP) or the nearest PHC/CHC.
  • Ensure all dates and names match exactly across all submitted documents.
  • Re-apply at Pehchan Portal with corrected documents.

Post-application, many experience errors in the final certificate (incorrect names, ages, or dates of death), which require further time-consuming corrections.

How to fix:

  • Prepare a Notarised Affidavit on stamp paper explaining the error and stating the correct information.
  • Submit the affidavit with the original certificate and supporting documents to the issuing ULB or Gram Panchayat office.
  • Request a corrected certificate — the Registrar is obligated to issue one on receipt of valid proof.
  • Track at Pehchan Portal or contact helpline 181.

In rural areas, lack of proper infrastructure and staff causes significant delays and some registrations remain "pending" for months.

How to fix:

  • Follow up in person at the Gram Panchayat or ULB office weekly until the application is processed.
  • Escalate to the Block Development Officer (BDO) or SDM in writing if pending beyond 30 days.
  • Raise a grievance via Rajasthan Sampark Portal or call 181.
